{{Quote|I'm not risking my crew for some fool girl intent on suicide!|Dakar, during Vima Sunrider's ordeal|<ref name="redemption1" />}}
'''Dakar''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Spaceport/Legends|port]] [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]] and ion [[Mining/Legends|miner]] on [[Exis Station/Legends|Exis Station]], active in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Along with being a leader of mining expeditions in the [[Teedio system/Legends|Teedio system]], Dakar was in charge of the [[Space station/Legends|space station's]] [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bay. He was good friends with the [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] [[Hoggon]] and cared about the safety of his crew despite his gruff and sometimes abrasive demeanor. In [[3986 BBY]], during a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Conclave on Exis Station|conclave]] on the station, a bored [[Padawan/Legends|Jedi apprentice]], [[Vima Sunrider]], joined Dakar's mining crew on an expedition to a [[Teedio|nearby]] [[Star/Legends|star]] without his knowledge. Sunrider's inexperience nearly doomed her, as she got caught in a [[magnetic loop]] and was nearly pulled into the sun, but Dakar refused to go after her, as he did not want to put his men in harm's way. Luckily for Sunrider, she was saved by a passing Jedi, [[Tott Doneeta]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Idiot! Can't you see that storm cell boiling in the chromosphere?|Dakar, to Vima Sunrider|<ref name="redemption1" />}}
Dakar worked as a<ref name="redemption1" /> [[Spaceport/Legends|port]] [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]]<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|I|162|Dakar}}</ref> and ion [[Mining/Legends|miner]] on [[Exis Station/Legends|Exis Station]], a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] in the [[Teedio system/Legends|Teedio system]], in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. A leader among [[Gender/Legends|his]] fellow miners, Dakar was in charge of mining expeditions to the nearby [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Teedio]], flying an [[ion mining ship]] during these missions.<ref name="redemption1" /> Dakar's duties also included directing incoming [[Starship/Legends|ships]] to their correct docking spaces in the station's [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bay. Over the years, Dakar developed a friendship with a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] named [[Hoggon]].<ref name="redemption2" /> In [[3986 BBY]], during a massive [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Conclave on Exis Station|conclave]] on Exis Station, Dakar led his crew on a seemingly-routine [[Plasma/Legends|plasma]] mining assignment—unbeknownst to Dakar, on that occasion they were joined by [[Vima Sunrider]], a Human [[Padawan/Legends|Jedi apprentice]] who had sneaked away from the [[Jedi Convocation]] due to boredom.<ref name="redemption1" />

Dakar first sensed something was awry when Sunrider's ship drifted out of formation, causing the veteran miner to snap angrily at her over the [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. Soon afterward, the inexperienced Sunrider became caught in a [[magnetic loop]], which threatened to pull her into the sun. Sunrider ditched her ship in an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]], but Dakar refused to retrieve her, as he would not risk his crew for such a dangerous rescue effort. As it turned out, Dakar did not need to save her, as a passing ship, flown by [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Tott Doneeta]], attached a cable to Sunrider's hurtling life-pod and pulled her out of harm's way.<ref name="redemption1" /> After the Sunrider incident, Dakar returned to his docking bay duties on Exis Station. He soon met with his old friend Hoggon, who came to Exis Station to observe the Jedi conclave, which by then had gone on for days. Hoggon, who held a fascination with the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], asked Dakar to direct him to a place where he could watch the proceedings; Dakar suggested an air shaft that looked down into the gathering hall. Hoggon paid Dakar several [[Coin/Legends|coins]] for use of his docking space, and the two friends parted ways.<ref name="redemption2" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|But what do you care, Hoggon? You're no Jedi.|Dakar, to his old friend Hoggon|<ref name="redemption2" />}}
Dakar had a gruff manner and was quick to admonish those who erred on his mining missions. However, he cared about the safety of his crew and would not put them in harm's way in situations where it was not warranted.<ref name="redemption1" /> Dakar had a long friendship with Hoggon, although he did not understand the spacer's interest in the Jedi Order. Dakar had [[white]] skin and [[black]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], with a mouthful of large [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]].<ref name="redemption2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dakar appeared as a minor character in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Redemption|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - Redemption'']], the final story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and released in [[1998]]. His sole appearances in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] to date, Dakar was featured in the arc's [[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 1|first]]<ref name="redemption1" /> and [[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 2|second]] issues, wherein he was illustrated by [[Chris Gossett]].<ref name="redemption2" /> In [[2008]], Dakar was given an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="cswe" />

==Appearances==
